content:
# GPU Grading Utilisation Measurement (2026-04-03)

Measured `grade_frame_cuda` performance via `nvidia-smi dmon` during Criterion benchmark run.

## Hardware
RTX 3060 Laptop GPU, 6144 MB VRAM

## Baseline measurement
- **~83 ms/frame at 1080p** (Criterion `grading/with_grader/1080p`)
- SM utilisation: 10–20%
- Memory bandwidth: 1–3%

## Root cause
`grade_frame_cuda` performs 14 per-frame `cudaMalloc`-backed operations:
- 8× `htod_sync_copy` (each = alloc + async copy + stream.synchronize())
- 6× `alloc_zeros` (explicit cudaMalloc)
- 1× `dtoh_sync_copy`

Each `cudaMalloc` costs ~3–8 ms on this GPU. Actual kernel compute (LUT trilinear + HSL + 3-pass clarity blur) is <1 ms at 1080p. ~70 ms of the 83 ms frame time is allocation overhead.

## Expected after fix
~6–10 ms/frame at 1080p after buffer pre-allocation (cudaMalloc eliminated; 8 `htod_sync_copy_into` sync barriers remain, contributing ~3–6 ms total).
